mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and equity sell-offs within one week as investors price in higher recession probabilities.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the signal fails to predict returns when tested out-of-sample over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals persistent capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the next month as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above 115, or the signal loses predictive power once GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP revisions and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.1 over a 10-year sample, or the signal generates negative returns in > 50% of positive GDP surprise instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing away from equities, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS sustains levels above 4.5% for consecutive quarters, or the threshold signal shows no statistical significance in a logistic regression of return direction.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of robust nonfarm payrolls growth and accelerating consumer credit expansion confirms a self-reinforcing demand cycle that lifts corporate cash flows and drives positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts following a policy shock signals rapid private sector recovery, triggering short-covering and momentum buying that lifts S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when HOUST rises above 1600, or the housing-driven momentum signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year walk-forward test.
